title: Tearing chest pain radiating to the back in a 62-year-old
presentation: A 62-year-old male with long-standing hypertension presents with sudden onset severe tearing chest pain radiating to the interscapular back, starting abruptly while lifting. He describes it as the worst pain ever. Vitals: BP 188/102 right arm, BP 154/88 left arm, HR 96, RR 20, SpO2 97%. Exam: aortic regurgitation murmur, normal neurological exam, pulses diminished in left arm.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: BP differential >20mmHg between arms + tearing back pain + aortic regurgitation = Type A dissection until proven otherwise. Do NOT give thrombolytics — fatal if dissection. Target HR <60 and SBP 100-120 with IV labetalol. Type A = surgical emergency.
